leftover.quote: The phase 1 controller core reconciles coalesced demand through a bounded worker
leftover.rationale: Narrates build history by numbered development phase. A first-time user has no context for 'phase 1' and it exposes internal staging.
leftover.suggested_action: Rewrite the entire intro paragraph (lines 8-19) in present tense as a flat feature list with no phase numbers: the controller reconciles coalesced demand through a bounded worker pool via explicit demand-source and runner-backend ports; ships a checksummed offline-built reference VM image and a versioned one-shot guest contract; runs periodic ownership inventory over an Incus backend (create/start/payload injection/observation/diagnostics/deletion); resolves a persistent GitHub scale set with message polling, demand statistics, and per-VM JIT config; supports hot-standby replacement and restart reconciliation; and hardens unattended operation with GitHub session recovery, bounded shutdown escalation, and a protected systemd deployment.

leftover.rationale: 'Phase 2' is a build milestone; the reference image and guest contract are shipped features.

leftover.rationale: 'Phase 3' is a build milestone; ownership inventory and the Incus backend are shipped.
leftover.suggested_action: Drop 'Phase 3 adds'; describe periodic ownership inventory and the Incus backend as present features. Also drop 'real' (contrasts with an earlier fake backend — internal framing).

leftover.rationale: The fallback reason string labels the tool the "v1 repository configurator." This is genuine user-facing output: extract_unsupported_settings() feeds UnsupportedSetting.reason, which print_report() writes to stdout and the --json-report file. A fresh operator sees an internal versioned-milestone label rather than a scope description. The nine curated entries in UNSUPPORTED_REASONS (lines 60-70) avoid any version framing, so this fallback is inconsistent with the tool's own vocabulary. Line number is correct.
leftover.suggested_action: Replace the fallback string with a version-free scope statement matching the style of the UNSUPPORTED_REASONS entries, e.g. "This setting is not applied by the repository configurator." (drop the "v1" token only; leave the surrounding .get() call unchanged).

Line number is correct.

leftover.rationale: Filename encodes internal build "phase 5". Referenced at README.md:200.
leftover.suggested_action: git mv to a functional name (e.g. hot-standby-proof.sh) and update README.md:200.

leftover.rationale: Default evidence directory name bakes in the "phase5" protocol label.
leftover.suggested_action: Rename default to hot-standby-evidence. Also update README.md:207, which passes /var/log/incus-gh-runner/phase5-hot-standby as this argument.

leftover.rationale: This is an EXTERNALLY VISIBLE GitHub Actions run title (surfaced in the Actions UI). It embeds internal "Phase 5" numbering. Critically, it must match .github/workflows/runner-functional.yml:2 (run-name: Phase 5 runner proof (...)) exactly, or the run-lookup jq filter at lines 255-257 fails.
leftover.suggested_action: Rename to a phase-neutral title (e.g. "Hot-standby runner proof (${correlation_id})") AND make the identical change to run-name in .github/workflows/runner-functional.yml:2 so the two strings stay byte-identical.

leftover.rationale: Generated correlation/proof ID embeds the "phase5" protocol label; it flows into evidence manifests and the dispatched workflow's proof_id input, so the label propagates into recorded artifacts.
leftover.suggested_action: Change the prefix to a neutral token, e.g. proof_id="hot-standby-$(date -u +%Y%m%dT%H%M%SZ)-$$".

leftover.rationale: Status message narrates the internal "phase 5" build-protocol label.
leftover.suggested_action: Rewrite as printf 'hot-standby proof passed; evidence: %s\n' "$evidence_directory".

leftover.rationale: Literal REPLACE_ME_... placeholder token is unambiguous template-scaffolding residue left in the commented-out rust example inherited from the repo template.
leftover.suggested_action: Remove the commented rust example block (lines 8-10) entirely; the file already explains all toolchains are mise-managed. If an example must stay as docs, substitute a real version string for the REPLACE_ME token.

leftover.rationale: The section header explicitly frames the following entries as artifacts of the AI-agent / session-driven development workflow. This framing is the actual leak of the internal process, independent of whether the ignore rules stay.
leftover.suggested_action: Rename the heading to something neutral, e.g. "# Local tooling and editor state".

missed.quote: `incus-gh-runner` is an early-stage controller for running ephemeral GitHub
missed.reason: 'early-stage' is development-status/WIP framing in the very first sentence a reader sees. Combined with the phase narrative that follows, it presents the product as unfinished scaffolding rather than a shipped tool.
missed.suggested_action: Drop 'early-stage' (or move honest maturity signaling to the existing SECURITY.md 'until the first stable release' language). Describe it plainly, e.g. 'incus-gh-runner is a controller for running ephemeral GitHub Actions jobs in Incus virtual machines.'

missed.quote: Actions jobs in Incus virtual machines. The controller will consume GitHub
missed.reason: Future-tense framing ('The controller will consume ... maintain ... and delete', lines 4-6) describes core shipped behavior as not-yet-built, which is WIP narrative inconsistent with the phase paragraph claiming these features are done.
missed.suggested_action: Convert to present tense: 'The controller consumes GitHub runner scale-set demand, maintains a bounded pool of hot-standby runners, and deletes each VM after its one assigned job.'

missed.quote: The proof drives one unit of fake demand through the controller, injects a
missed.reason: 'The proof' has no antecedent for a first-time reader and ties back to the deleted 'hardware proof' phase narrative; it reads as internal proof-run vocabulary for what is simply the Incus lifecycle functional test.
missed.suggested_action: Refer to it as the test, e.g. 'The test drives one unit of fake demand through the controller, ...', consistent with renaming the surrounding phase-numbered artifacts.

missed.quote: locked development toolchain, CI gates, and isolated GitHub and Incus client adapters
missed.reason: The scanner's line-20 finding only flags "renamed CLI". The rest of the same sentence describes internal repo/build infrastructure ("locked development toolchain", "CI gates", "repository foundation") that is scaffolding-status framing, not product a user of the controller needs. A first-time reader sees the project described by its dev-tooling state.
missed.suggested_action: Delete the "current repository foundation provides the ... toolchain, CI gates, ..." sentence (lines 20-21) entirely, or reduce to at most a single product-relevant sentence. "isolated GitHub and Incus client adapters" is architecture and could stay only if reframed as a current design fact rather than repo-foundation status.

missed.quote: The checked-in systemd deployment loads the GitHub App key as a service credential
missed.reason: "checked-in" (also "The checked-in unit" in deployment.md line 8) narrates that the artifact lives in the repo, a development-process framing rather than describing behavior. Lower confidence than the phase items but a first-time reader gains nothing from "checked-in".
missed.suggested_action: Drop "checked-in"; say "The systemd deployment loads the GitHub App key as a service credential ...". Apply the same change to deployment.md line 8 ("The checked-in unit" -> "The provided unit" or "The unit").

missed.quote: Boot validation below remains the functional acceptance gate.
missed.reason: Borderline: "acceptance gate" is test/dev-process jargon. It conveys a real point (boot validation is the authoritative check), so it is not clearly scaffolding, but the wording reads as internal QA process language. Flagging for reviewer judgment.
missed.suggested_action: Optionally soften to plain language, e.g. "Boot validation below is the authoritative functional check." Leave as-is if "acceptance gate" is considered acceptable product wording.

missed.reason: The local shell variable `proof_dir` carries the same 'proof' phase-narrative framing and was not itemized in the scanner's downstream-rename list (which named description, env var, group key, and evidence-file field but omitted this variable). It is used on lines 46, 47, 52, and 58.
missed.suggested_action: Rename the shell variable to a neutral name such as `evidence_dir` (or `work_dir`) and update all references on lines 46, 47, 52, and 58 alongside the `proof_id` -> `correlation_id` rename.

missed.quote: mise REPLACES Proto (the former .prototools + .moon/proto/*). The committed
missed.reason: Historical migration narrative: references Proto, .prototools, and .moon/proto/* — internal tooling that never ships in this public repo, so a first-time reader has no context. This is exactly the 'historical build narrative / migration storytelling' the sweep targets, and the scanner did not flag it.
missed.suggested_action: Rewrite to describe current behavior only, dropping the Proto contrast, e.g. 'mise manages tool + environment versions. The committed mise.lock records per-platform download URLs + checksums (and cosign/SLSA/GitHub-attestation verification for aqua-backed tools).'

missed.reason: Migration narrative ('now ... instead of moonrepo/setup-toolchain') describing how the build evolved rather than its current state; unflagged by the scanner.
missed.suggested_action: State the current setup without the 'now/instead of' framing, e.g. 'moon is installed via mise; melange/apko/cosign drive the container build and image signing in the release workflow.'

missed.quote: Docs (MkDocs) toolchain — moved off moon's former unstable_python/unstable_uv.
missed.reason: Migration narrative referencing removed moon features (unstable_python/unstable_uv); historical build storytelling with no value to a first-time reader.
missed.suggested_action: Drop the 'moved off ... former' clause; describe current usage, e.g. 'Docs (MkDocs) toolchain via uv.'

missed.reason: Uses 'the former ...' historical framing implying a prior shipped build. Lower priority than the phase leaks because the distroless-equivalence rationale is genuinely useful to users evaluating the image; flagged for consistency with the sweep's historical-narrative target.
missed.suggested_action: Drop 'former' and state the posture directly, e.g. 'a distroless-equivalent nonroot posture (uid 65532, ca-certs, tzdata, no shell).'

missed.quote: the binary via -ldflags, mirroring GoReleaser and the former Dockerfile.
missed.reason: References a Dockerfile that no longer exists in the repo — mild historical build narrative; unflagged by the scanner.
missed.suggested_action: Drop 'and the former Dockerfile' (e.g. '... via -ldflags, mirroring GoReleaser.'). Same for the parallel 'former Dockerfile' phrasing if any callers reference it.

missed.quote: blackbox-only is worth the export pollution.
missed.reason: Mild forward-looking future-work framing ('Re-enable if/when the test layout grows large enough ...') for the disabled testpackage linter. Borderline: it is legitimate config rationale, but carries the 'if/when it grows' future narrative the sweep watches for. Optional.
missed.suggested_action: Optional: trim the 'Re-enable if/when ... grows large enough' future clause and keep only the current rationale for disabling testpackage, or leave as-is if the team accepts config rationale comments.

uncertain.rationale: Functional ignore for a local agent working directory. The name reveals the agent workflow, but the rule usefully prevents committing local state. Keep-or-remove depends on whether that tooling is still used for the public repo.
uncertain.suggested_action: Keep the rule (move it under the neutral heading from finding #22) if contributors still use the tooling; delete the line only if .agents/ is retired post-release.

uncertain.rationale: The directory name directly matches the internal session-journal protocol term from .session.md/CLAUDE.md, so it reveals the workflow. It is a tool-owned path that cannot be renamed, and the rule prevents committing journal state.
uncertain.suggested_action: Keep silently under the neutral heading while journaling is used; remove the line only if the journaling protocol is dropped for the public repo.

uncertain.rationale: Matches the Worktrunk (wt) working-tree term used by the internal branching workflow; reveals the process but is a tool-owned path that must be ignored while Worktrunk is used.
uncertain.suggested_action: Keep silently under the neutral heading while Worktrunk is used; remove only if that tooling is retired for the public repo.
